provenance.source_corpus: Short‐term response of fish communities and water chemistry to breaching of a causeway in the Sarita River Estuary, British Columbia, Canada

source_spans.text: a causeway isolating the southern portion of the Sarita River estuary was breached in 2018
enhance juvenile salmon access through the causeway
breaching was effective in increasing connectivity of the Sarita Estuary
Chinook and Chum Salmon utilization increased inside and outside of the causeway following breaching
Chinook and Chum Salmon utilization increased inside and outside of the causeway following breaching
Water chemistry was homogenized post-breach
Salinity inside and outside of the causeway homogenized post-breach compared to pre-breach
Water temperature consistently increased in both pools post-breach relative to pre-breach sampling
Measurements at surface of pH in both the Freshwater and Saltwater Pools increased during the post-breach period
Three-spined Sticklebacks decreased inside the causeway
Prickly Sculpins decreased both inside and outside the causeway
After breaching, Bay Pipefish decreased substantially inside of the causeway

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:3ad7761996e9
edges.original_sentence: breaching was effective in increasing connectivity of the Sarita Estuary, allowing access to 1.4 km 2 of habitat that had previously been inaccessible to juvenile salmon.

source_spans.text: breaching was effective in increasing connectivity of the Sarita Estuary, allowing access to 1.4 km 2 of habitat that had previously been inaccessible to juvenile salmon.
